name: Drakheir The First Hand Tracking ARPG

reviews.review_description: The casting is fun and enjoyable through the game. I do look forward to looking back at this in the future after some promised patching is done to the locomotion system. The story seems intresting and I really want to get deeper involved if only to run around firing off  Finger guns (fireballs) and Ice ninja stars. The Mini Games are a good time as well really liked the Wack a Mole one alot and was able to get into it despite my issues with movement. Hope to see the game fully developed and deserving of an increased rating.

reviews.review_title: THE HAND-TRACKING RPG I WAS WAITING FOR!
reviews.review_description:
Drakheir is one of the most unique VR experiences I've played.
What started as an ambitious hand-tracking project has grown into a genuine fantasy adventure where your hands truly become your weapons. Casting spells with gestures, summoning abilities, and interacting with the world without controllers creates a level of immersion that feels fresh and magical.
The game deserves praise for pushing hand tracking far beyond a simple tech demo. Exploring the fantasy world, battling enemies, mastering new powers, and learning different magical combinations is both fun and rewarding.
What impressed me most is the passion behind the project. The developers took a bold idea and transformed it into a real action RPG experience that showcases the potential of hand tracking in VR.
If you've ever dreamed of feeling like a wizard casting spells with your own hands, Drakheir delivers that fantasy beautifully.
Congratulations to the team for creating one of the most innovative hand-tracking adventures available on Quest. A fantastic experience that every VR enthusiast should try.

I love the game but I wish it had more spells or at the very least be able to cast spells by saying certain keywords or phrases by voice but overall love the game can't wait to see what's the next update is

Drakheir completely surprised me. This is one of the best experiences I’ve ever had on the Quest 3, and easily one of the most impressive uses of full hand-tracking today. The amount of freedom, creativity, and variety packed into this game goes far beyond what I expected from an indie title.
You’re not just casting spells—you’re mastering them. More than 30 unique abilities, each with powerful upgrades, elemental combinations, and creative effects. From summoning minions to calling down meteor showers, forming tornadoes, shaping blades, or firing elemental guns… it all works, and it feels incredibly satisfying, Movement is smooth and fluid using only your hands, combat feels dynamic without ever becoming frustrating, and the environments are beautiful and surprisingly large. .

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your incredible review! We're thrilled to hear that you're enjoying the hand-tracking and creative freedom in Drakheir. It was our goal to create a truly immersive RPG experience on the Quest , and your feedback means the world to our team. Happy spellcasting

reviews.review_description: I really like this game but i wish it was a little more complex, like it has 4 maps and the magic is cool but i was expecting a little more

developer_response.body: Thank you for your favorable comments, we will continue to update some new maps and playing methods in the future

reviews.review_description: Add multiplayer please

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment. We will continue to update our content

reviews.review_title: More than a tech demo, less than a game
reviews.review_description:
The core gameplay of Drakheir is pretty fun due to the novelty of casting spells with Naruto hand signs, but it’s not nearly enough to make me recommend this game.
I could not tell you what the story of this game is actually supposed to be, as you are dropped into the world with no explained backstory or goals. The whole time I was scrambling for any plot details to be told to me. Not to mention the time loop concept making things more complex.
Eventually you just start playing the whole game in reverse with no level design changes, not that the level design is really notable anyways.
Also the ending of the game just… happens. Really suddenly it’s just over without any real big finale.
Getting back to the core gameplay it’s the one thing that kept me engaged for a bit as it can feel pretty fun, but eventually you notice the total lack of complexity. You can just throw fireballs the whole game and you will not be punished. There is no reason to use most of the spells besides boredom.

For hand tracking, this game is ridiculous, i love it so much and i bought it today. spent 3 hours repairing my controllers cuz they had stick drift and after i finish my brother who helped me said “we spent 3 hours fixing these so you can play a hand tracking game” that’s how much i love this game, its so fun. i just hope i dont get bored of it

This was so much fun! I noticed that I needed to restart the game if I’ve been running for more than 30 minutes but other than that, even with minor glitches, I absolutely loved the accuracy of the hand tracking and the signs. I felt so bad@$$

Its a verry good Game. It have a lot of spells and combinations, that are cool!

Two critics:

First, its a bit to easy
Second, the graphics are not so good


But its … Wow !

reviews.review_title: Top class game! God like feeling!
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing this game for months and i really feel like i owe it a good review cause this is a fantastic game that many years ago i was wishing it could have existed.

It definitely sets the standard in the VR gaming industry as there is nothing else like this right now and i am not talking only about the hand tracking but also about its unique style and visuals that brings all the joy and smile to my face while i am playing it.
All these insane powers at your hands together with an amazing storyline makes you feel like living the dream of being a superhero.

There might be few bugs or imperfections here and there but my appreciation and respect for this project is so big that i can only enjoy what i am given so far.

Right now what this game needs in order to shine is some upscaling to its resolution as it looks quite blurry on my meta quest 3. Thankfully with the quest game optimiser app this can be fixed however there are no official profiles for this game yet and i have to experiment to find the proper one. Hopefully there will be an official solution for this matter in the near future.
I was expecting this game to be amazing but I couldn’t get most of the spells to work and the accuracy of the signs you need to do with your hands had to be perfect, I AM GETTING A REFUND ASAP

reviews.review_title: A Very Refreshing Change of Pace
reviews.review_description:
This game is officially in my top 3 now. It is absolutely fantastic, from the graphics being really well done for it still being a relatively newer game, to the soundtrack which ranges from calming ambiance to bass phonk boss battle tunes. As far as the gameplay, it's super smooth and really intuitive. There were things I just sort of did without thinking about what I was doing, and every time something cool happened in response. There is really a lot of thought and effort put into this game. 11/10 Highly recommend. 

Don't listen to any negativity if you are considering trying this game. It brings a whole new aspect of VR capability to the table via hand tracking only for the whole game. It brings back that excitmentni felt from my first time in vr and thinking of all the possibilities for future gaming. This is definitely it, the next step. Now go get in there folks, it's a blast!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your incredible review! It truly means the world to us to hear that Drakheir has made it into your top three games. We’ve poured our hearts into making this a unique and immersive experience, so knowing that you’re enjoying everything from the graphics and soundtrack to the intuitive gameplay is beyond rewarding.

We especially love that you mentioned how natural and exciting the gameplay feels, our goal has always been to make hand tracking feel like second nature, and it’s amazing to hear that it clicked so well for you.

Your enthusiasm and support fuel us to keep improving and expanding the game. We’ve got even more exciting updates on the way, so stay tuned for future enhancements! If you ever want to connect with the community or share your experiences, we’d love to have you in our Discord at discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU

Thanks again for your support, and we hope you continue having an absolute blast in Drakheir!

reviews.review_description:
There really isn't anything on the market quite like this, so it really has no competition. That's why I'm giving it five stars. For it's innovation. The hand tracking is as good as you'd want it to be. Only thing that is awkward is grabbing movement but honestly it also makes the most sense with those limitations.


However moving is awkward. I'm going to get a VR treadmill eventually. Will this game support vr treadmill? I feel like hand controls with vr treadmill movement would lessen the weaknesses. However I get it, at that point that's a pretty niche crowd to cater to.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the 5-star review! We’re thrilled that you appreciate the uniqueness and innovation behind Drakheir. Hand tracking is something we’ve worked hard on, so it’s great to hear it meets expectations. We understand that movement can feel a bit awkward, and we’re always looking at ways to refine it within the limitations of the system.  

As for VR treadmill support, that’s an interesting idea! It’s not something we currently have implemented, but we’ll definitely take it into consideration. As you said, it’s a niche setup, but if there’s enough demand, it could be worth exploring. Our developers are very new to the tech and don't currently have access to one, though we might be able to see what we can do with the current engines capabilities that's already built in and adapt it to Drakheir.

If you want to stay updated on future improvements or chat with the community about ideas like this, feel free to join our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU. Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_description: This game is so cool! I feel like I have so much power! This game is truly awesome and I definitely recommend. I can’t wait to see what the game has next. Also, is it true there is a mixed reality mode? If so, how do you do it? This game is so cool!
A bit rough on the edge but very innovative.

As promised I have posted my first impressions  on my channel realchillgaming on this truly one of a kind vr game I mean rpg full story a good mix of bad guys great voice acting and it's all handtracking need I say more besides this game is Awesome if your a fan of handtracking like I am forgot to change or charge your remotes or just need a new way to play well this may just be the game for you hopes this helps laterz

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for sharing your first impressions and for featuring Drakheir on your channel, RealChillGaming! We’re thrilled to hear that you’ve enjoyed the unique hand-tracking mechanics, the RPG elements, and the overall experience. It’s awesome to know that our game stood out to you in such a positive way, especially as a fan of hand-tracking. 

If you ever have more thoughts or feedback as you continue playing, we’d love to hear from you. Feel free to join our Discord community at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU to chat with us and share any ideas for future updates. 

Thanks again for the support, and we’re excited to keep bringing more awesome updates to Drakheir!

reviews.review_title: Nice game but with a lot of bugs
reviews.review_description: The game is so much fun and the way it reads hand motions is amazing but it has soooo many bugs that need to be fixed and I get bugged by how many times you have to wait for the screenplay evrrytime you die

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback! We’re really glad you’re enjoying the hand-tracking mechanics, but we hear you on the bugs and the wait times after dying. The team is actively working on fixes to improve stability and make the experience smoother.  

A big update is coming soon, and we hope it will address many of the issues you mentioned. If you feel the game improves after the update, we’d really appreciate it if you could consider updating your review. Also, feel free to join our Discord to stay up to date and share any other feedback: https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Thanks for playing Drakheir!

reviews.review_description:
It's my 10-year-old son's favourite game. He has a lot of fun playing with it. So for me this game reached the purpose for I bought meta quest 3: to entertain my 10-year-old child.

I can't imagine how much more fun it will be with a mixed reality mode.

Very good!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We are so glad that they enjoy it and yup MR modes are coming soon in a coming update! Your kind words mean a lot to us and we wish you and your family a lot of fun with VR!

reviews.review_title: It feels like it’s still in Alpha.
reviews.review_description: I really like the concept, and there are brilliant moments when everything works as intended. The gesture recognition is by far the most polished feature. However, the whole game feels more like an Alpha version than even a Beta. I waited to write my review until after the big update, and it has improved considerably. That said, I personally think it still needs several more updates like the last one. If they keep this up, it could become a really solid game. If you don’t mind encountering bugs and enjoy pulling off powerful combos with your hands, it can be quite entertaining.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful and detailed review of Drakheir. We’re thrilled to hear you see potential in the game and enjoyed moments when everything clicked, especially with the gesture recognition system. Your support and acknowledgment of the improvements after the last update mean a lot to our team.

We understand your concerns about the game still feeling like it’s in an early stage, and we’re actively working on updates to address these issues and refine the overall experience. If you have specific feedback or ideas on what we can improve to make Drakheir truly shine, we’d love to hear from you. Please consider joining our Discord community at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU so we can collaborate directly. 

If you feel the game has improved enough to warrant a higher review, we’d be incredibly grateful if you could update your review to reflect your experience with the latest version. Reviews like yours play a huge role in supporting small teams like ours, and they help us grow and reach more players.

Thank you again for your feedback and for giving Drakheir a chance. We’re committed to making it the best it can be, and your input helps make that possible.

reviews.review_title: Wanna feel like you're a Bad A**?
reviews.review_description:
This game makes you feel like you're a pro magic (gun)slinger, shooting fireballs from your fingertips (finger guns)! And with all the explosions the fire spells do, you feel like you're in a Western Movie directed by Michael Bay.

Now I'm not typically into fire magic, but man was it quite fun ( I also had a Ice Shurikens, those were fun too! but I was mostly using fire spells). I'm more into Necromancy should a game have it. Now, I haven't played much yet so I do not know if the game has anything Necromancy related, but luckily I found the game DOES have summons, as a wandering merchant sold me a Fire Golem! Man was I ecstatic to find out there was such a thing, I was NOT expecting there to be summons in such a fast paced game like this.

So far my experience, though short (since I get tired easily) was in my opinion "phenomenal". It tickled my brain in ways I cannot describe being able to just use my hands instead of controllers. 

I absolutely LOVE it! I know I'm just getting started too, I have yet to see all the game has to even offer! Lots of magic still to discover~

I will admit though, I did have some slight issues despite my high praises but I believe that is due to user error and my own poor lighting and not the games fault. When I was in combat it felt like it worked smooth as butter to me, my only "problem" was movement. But I think it's because I don't quite "grasp" how to do it very well. 

Maybe once I play more, get better and find better lighting will I be able to provide a bit more critical  insight. Otherwise, I VERY much enjoyed my time so far!


Despite all that! My Score, (according to JonTron)

https://youtu.be/LTvvwwCTCA0?si=Y3jNhkwhwNcKCOkN

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We will continue updating the game to reach its best state. We warmly invite you to join our Discord and welcome your input:https://discord.com/invite/SmEQk93M5E

reviews.review_description:
Stepping into the world of Drakheir VR was an exhilarating experience. The game's hand-tracking technology is impressively accurate, allowing for intuitive and immersive gameplay. Summoning weapons with a simple gesture and casting elemental spells with unique hand signs felt incredibly satisfying. The dynamic movement scheme took a bit of getting used to, but once I got the hang of it, exploring the richly detailed fantasy world was a joy. The ability to fuse magic spells added a layer of strategy that kept combat engaging and fresh.

However, there were a few hiccups. Occasionally, the hand-tracking would glitch, leading to some frustrating moments during intense battles. Additionally, the learning curve for mastering the hand signs and movement scheme can be steep, which might deter some players.

Overall, Drakheir VR is a groundbreaking experience that sets a new standard for hand-tracking in ARPGs. The immersion and depth of engagement are unparalleled, making it a must-play for VR enthusiasts. Despite its minor flaws, the game's innovative mechanics and rich world earn it a solid 5 out of 5.

developer_response.body: We really appreciate your finding the hand-tracking experience enjoyable and leaving this positive comment! We just want you to know that we are going to continue updating and polishing this build to the best possible state. If you have any requests or ideas on new game modes or want to report a bug. Please don't hesitate to join our discord community and share your thoughts/ideas. https://discord.com/invite/SmEQk93M5E

reviews.review_description:
Thank you for braving the frontier!

People go wild for haptics and different kinds of immersion, but even on the quest 2, let me say how immersive and appreciated it is just to be able to use my hands in such a consisted and deep way of playing the game

The skills keep building and are highly based and player chosen skills, and even with several hand denature spells unlocked, I can still consistently cast spells in high paced battle while pulling myself around the battlefield

The only thing I had to get used to was making sure my hands were roughly in view of the cameras, and that being checked, gameplay was consistent, deepf, immersive, and highly enjoyable enjoyableenjoyable

This game is a lot of fun. I was able to play sitting down in a chair so I could turn to move to the different areas though it may seem more suited for standing, sitting is also an option.  Arpgs are a pretty untapped area when it comes to hand tracking at it is nice to have something new and fresh.
    You can cast various spells by completing the symbols that show up on screen, with any game this comes more easy and natural the more you cast these spells. There are also weapons you can conjur and once unlocked by posing your hands in certain areas. The developers listen and truly engage feedback and continue to polish this title as time goes on. I can't wait to see what else comes from this game. Hand tracking just seems like a natural progression for VR and this is one of the more enjoyable hand tracking experiences that is available on the market. I hope to see more games in this style and would recommend this to anyone who wants to experience casting spells without being tethered to controllers. This is truly a unique experience.

developer_response.body: Hey hey! Thank you 10000 times for the kind words. It is the most encouraging thing through the journey to see you enjoying the game. Yes, we are planning new stuff for the next year's updates. Please don't hesitate to join our discord community and share your thoughts/ideas. https://discord.com/invite/SmEQk93M5E

reviews.review_title: Only real magic user game you need!
reviews.review_description: This game is absolutely amazing. Great graphics, great storyline, lots of action and a great challenge easily my top magic caster game to date. There's nothing like performing hand gestures to conjure things from the ether to wield and fight your foes. Being able to control the elements just by simple hand gestures is something Marvel characters could do. This game Bridges the gap. Amazing! Definitely a 10 out of 10

Drakheir is more than a game; it is one of those invites into an entire wild fantasy of mastering magic through hand-tracking. Since the very moment I stepped into its breathtakingly detailed world, I felt just like Quentin Coldwater from The Magicians-channeling arcane power with nothing but my gestures.
Where Drakheir really shines is in the controls: summoning a weapon by gesturing to grab an axe from resting on my shoulder to pulling a katana out of a sheath, weaving intricate hand signs to cast elemental spells-even fusing those elements in for devastating effects-it feels like second nature. Unfortunately this is also where the game needs some more polish. Movement felt a little buggy  as i'd rubberband as im pulling myself across the map,  it was still enjoyable even with that happening though!
Drakheir is a bold leap forward for hand-tracking technology, setting a new benchmark for immersion and depth. If you’ve ever dreamed of wielding magic with your bare hands, this is your chance to step into that fantasy and live it fully.

Drakheir feels a bit like an old school Final Fantasy game or Arcaxer but in first person view the entire game.  Combat revolves around timed attacks from the enemy that you can dodge and counter, all while popping off your magic at them.  The hand gestures make a lot of sense.  Want to summon an axe?  Hold your hands like you're two handing an axe handle.  Bring up a shield?  Hold your fist in front of you like you're holding a shield to block incoming attacks and it'll spawn.  I was initially concerned about having to memorize all of the hand gestures for combat, but they're so intuitive it's not an issue (and if you do forget you can look down at your waist for a quick reminder).  There are finisher style sequences where you make multiple hand gestures in a row to essentially execute a low health boss.  Movement is probably the best I've seen so far in a hand tracking game, you literally make a fist like you're grabbing the world and pull your hand towards you to pull yourself forward (as if you're pulling yourself along a rope).  There is a story to follow, and it's at least decent.

Drakheir is VERY intuitive to pickup and play so don't be hesitant based on the hand tracking aspect.  Yes, sometimes there are gesture recognition issues, but I attribute that more to the Quest hand tracking from the headset and not from the game itself as this is par for the course in every hand tracking game I've played so far.  I'm not sure if Drakheir is marketed as a fitness game, but it should be.  My legs felt the burn after playing for a decent chunk of time.  Dodging enemy attacks is essentially doing squats and lunges, but with the added benefit of being fun!

reviews.review_title: Feel like Avatar: The Last Air bender
reviews.review_description:
Let me save you some time… BUY IT! This is what VR is all about! 

 This game is so much fun! If you ever wanted to feel like you’re a fire, earth, wind or water bender (animated series) or some really cool anime character with super powers  — this game is the only game that I know help you feel that experience in a believable way . It’s actually remarkably responsive to your hand gestures & although the presentation is still being polished— the experience is one of a kind! Buy it & support these developers! This is already one of my favorite games! I’m looking for to any DLC or updates coming down the pipeline. Perhaps the sound design can have more punch but I’m really liking the sound effect for the super powers.

-Product received for free to TEST and review-
This product has a good concept, and a team of developers that are actively working to improve. They have released to places for people to playtest and review so they can improve so I rounded up to a second star. This will be updated if a future update improves enough to go up!

Consider this VERY early access and so long as you know that going in you might still be happy. The devs are friendly and responsive.

The concept of use the finger tracking to let you cast all the spells with hand gestures is fun, but alone is not enough to hold an entire game together. There are still a lot of core game issues in the copy I played  with loading, glitches, visual artifacts, voice/motion/subtitles not aligning, enemy spawns, etc.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to test and review Drakheir! We truly appreciate your feedback and your willingness to revisit the game as we continue improving it.  

As a small team working on our first title, we’re dedicated to refining the experience, and we have a major update coming soon that addresses many of the core issues you mentioned, including improved performance, bug fixes, and overall polish. We know that strong mechanics alone aren’t enough, and we’re actively working to enhance every aspect of the game.  

We’d love for you to check out the updates when they’re live and see how far the game has come. If you have any further suggestions or encounter issues, we’re always open to feedback on our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Thanks again for giving Drakheir a chance, and we hope to earn a higher rating from you in the future!

reviews.review_description:
Impact Labs tester here!
This game is fun to mess around in, once I unlocked other spells it got way more fun than casting just fireball, (most) of the spells are intuitive, and if they aren’t they have visual instructions on how to use them.
On unlock of every spell there is a small tutorial that teaches you how to use it.
The story is there, I didn’t quite follow but it’s some fantasy something or other 
The art style is nice… not sure if the way the women are represented is a positive or negative…
TL;DR Pros
Fun
Spells differ in all the ways that matter
Well thought out unlocking
Good art style and areas, way different biomes for each level, and they all look good.
Story is there for people that want it.

However, there are still some small issues, biggest ones first.
Spells are sometimes clunky, and other ones like the axe thunder thing are weird in design and usage, you do get used to them but they still throw me off.
Enemy AI is less smart than AI usually is.
Animations and models for enemies felt off and kinda generic if I was immersed, might just be because I’ve seen a lot of cartoony style games or models though.
Speaking of immersion I found it a little hard to take the game seriously, for me it works fine but for other they might not like it.  
Menus are quirky
TL;DR Cons
Things are… different, best adjective for it.
Some character models and animations felt generic to me, might not to others.
Lacked some polish.

Keep in mind this is written from playing in early access! Things will be better by the time you play, especially if you’re reading this in a couple months after release.

TL;DR Overall
Fun game, fresh, and a good experience that hand tracking didn’t have.
Some things are a little clunky or confusing, sometimes both.

Overall it’s nice to play, bet this would be something cool to show family or friends who don’t have VR, and I would recommend if you’re into fantasy, or even not, I still found it enjoyable.

7.5-8/10

As a game in general, I’d probably give this one a 3. It just feels too generic in the characters, story and terrain while trying to be a fantasy RPG. It feels like older console level graphics and animations with short scenes of minor combat in between. However, being that it is one of the first of its kind taking hand tracking to a whole new level, I give it a little slack. The hand commands to cast spells are pretty awesome. It really does make you feel like you are there truly casting spells like you see them do in games while you were actually just pushing buttons. I know the actual mechanics of the hand tracking are still relying on the Quest to make it work properly, but it still needs work to make it reliable. It felt like at times, no matter how I held my hands, I just wasn't getting the gestures the game was expecting or it just cast something completely different. Then, don’t relax your hands out of sight, because then you might just get a floating hand in your face. There are bits of the game that just seem like they were just thrown together as it snaps you in place or what you think is expected to happen just vanishes. Then performing a finisher does feel fun, but you might not realize it is waiting for a specific position for one enemy while you are wondering why everything is frozen looking at another. I feel as hand tracking gets better, more things like this game can happen, but it would be a lot better if it also felt like more effort was in the rest of the game as well.

reviews.review_title: Not one for hand tracking game but...
reviews.review_description: I an not really one for hand tracking game, at all, but I really liked this one and how it handled the hand tracking, I like the attacks, movement, and the gameplay, this game does the hand tracking really nicely and is one of the best games I have seen for hand tracking, I really like the snowfight and wack the moel mini games, but I really! Like the Main story, 4-5 stars on the hand tracking and story, and I can't wait to play more of it later,

reviews.review_title: A Revolutionary Leap in Hand Tracking
reviews.review_description:
Drakheir: The First Hand Tracking ARPG is a groundbreaking addition to the world of virtual reality gaming, offering an unparalleled immersive experience that truly sets it apart from standard tech demos. From the moment you step into its richly detailed fantasy world, you realize that this game is not just about playing; it's about living the adventure.

The innovative use of hand tracking technology allows players to engage in combat and exploration in a way that feels natural and instinctive. With just a flick of your wrist or a simple gesture, you can summon a variety of weapons, creating a dynamic and engaging combat experience. The new movement scheme is a game-changer, allowing you to maneuver in ways that feel fluid and intuitive, making every action deeply satisfying.

One of the standout features is the ability to cast unique hand signs to unleash elemental spells. The fusion of these spells adds another layer of strategy, enabling players to become true masters of the elements. The challenges posed by powerful bosses require not only skillful maneuvers but also smart strategies, making every victory feel well-earned. And yes, you even have the chance to face off against a dragon, which is as thrilling as it sounds!

While Drakheir has already made significant strides in redefining hand-tracking experiences, it does have some minor issues that need polishing. However, what sets this game apart is the dedication of the developers, who are actively working to enhance the gameplay and address these concerns. Their commitment to continuous improvement is evident, and it makes me excited to see how the game will evolve over time.

Drakheir is a really interesting game that focuses on hand tracking to control the game and combat. Taking place in a fantasy setting with incredible monsters and lush locations it tries something different and offers players a fresh way of experiencing a new and exciting world.

Spells require different hand movements and signs to cast, and made me feel very powerful. Placing me in a world that I felt like I could take on even the biggest foes. The art style and direction take many fantasy tropes and explores them on a bit more than surface level, but the story did leave me a bit confused with some of the writing. Maybe it’s that the developer might be from a different country, but some of the writing left me a bit puzzled.

The minigames available in between checkpoints was nice and there seemed to be a larger focus on variety in those sections than the combat arenas. I enjoyed the combat and casting spells with signs but I did have a couple occasions where I felt I was making the sign but it did not recognize it. 

Darkheir is a game that I would have loved as a kid growing up, with the hand sign casting of spells making me feel extra powerful, like I could take on the world. It’s a solid game, and well worth a try.

reviews.review_title: Nice trip into the world of magic
reviews.review_description:
Drakheir is an magic-based game with a truly awesome concept. It lets you cast a wide variety of spells using just your bare hands, thanks to intuitive gesture-based controls. The spell repertoire includes some truly cool options, like summoning magic ice katanas, launching fireballs (activated with playful finger-gun gestures!), or creating protective magic shields.

Graphics are pretty solid imo - The whole game is kept in a vibrand comic-like graphic-style which is a pretty convincing and coherent. The character art reminded me a bit of Warcraft / Dota characters. Sound design is good in my opinion. Story is told in on screen dialogues and nicely done cutscenes.

However there are a few bugs here and there which needed to be fixed and the game need a last bit of polishing to be perfect but it is still a very good choice if you are looking for a magic /wizard game.

As stated in the title of the game, this is a hand-tracked ARPG. I was skeptical of how well they'd be able to pull off a fully hand-tracked game, but I can confidently say they nailed it. It is incredibly satisfying to pull off really quick spells and the combos are super fun. There are also many cool sections with huge creatures/environments. It was a bit hard to follow the story and some of the character animations are a bit stiff, but the gameplay was superb. Would definitely recommend to anybody looking for a cool hand-tracked game.

reviews.review_title: Cool concept But not for me
reviews.review_description: Listen, I love magic games especially with hand tracking, but this just wasn’t it. The AI voice acting Is ok at first, but I cannot stand to hear another word come out of one of the characters voice again￼￼￼ the combat seemed a little bland and repetitive and you can tell they did not have a big budget for this game, which is completely fine and I hope to see the game grow ￼￼in the future but I would not pay $15 for this ￼maybe 5

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate you giving Drakheir a try and sharing your honest thoughts. As a small indie team working on our first game, we know there are areas that can be improved, and we’re actively working on making the experience better.  

We have a major update coming soon that enhances combat, improves overall polish, and adds more variety to the gameplay. We hear you on the AI voice acting and will continue to explore ways to refine the game’s immersion.  

We truly hope you’ll revisit Drakheir after the update and see how far it has come. If you have specific feedback or ideas, we’d love to hear from you on our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Your input means a lot, and we’re committed to making Drakheir the best it can be. Thank you for giving it a shot!

reviews.review_description:
Drakheir is a wild experience. The entire game is played using hand tracking to cast spells. Drakheir also has a wonderful and ambitious way of tackling movement using only hand tracking. When you get the hang of casting each spell, it is really immersive to shift your hands around and unleash some serious damage. As a bonus, I found myself getting a lot of exercise while playing. 

Fans of fitness and fantasy will love this. It is also worth checking out simply because of the risk the developers took to make some exciting exclusive control methods. My only complaints would be the story and the visuals. I felt like the gameplay was solid enough not to make up for it. I would definitely recommend it to anybody gaming on the quest.

Good luck, magicians!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! Please consider giving it a 5-star review to encourage devs to buy more energy drinks. We will continue updating the game to reach its best state. We warmly invite you to join our Discord and solve issues with you:https://discord.com/invite/SmEQk93M5E

reviews.review_description: Boring and repetitive. Tracking is poor, and it looks bad... even for a quest game

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience with Drakheir hasn’t met your expectations. As a small team, we’re constantly working to improve, and honest reviews like yours are crucial in helping us understand where we need to focus our efforts.

If you’re open to it, we’d love for you to join our Discord community at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU to share more detailed thoughts on what didn’t work for you. We’re always listening and eager to learn how we can make the game better, whether it’s addressing tracking issues, enhancing visuals, or improving gameplay variety.

Your feedback could make a huge difference in shaping future updates, and we’d be grateful for the chance to turn your experience into a more positive one. Thank you for giving Drakheir a try.

reviews.review_description:
Drakheir is a fun ARPG with a unique hand tracking control scheme. Control different spells, attacks, conversation, and movement without the use of controllers as you progress along the story.

Although movement can take a little to get used to without being able to use the control stick of the touch controllers, the gestures that you make when casting spells and attacks are definitely the star of the show. As you upgrade different moves, you’ll learn new attacks and abilities to progress through the story. The fidelity at which the quest recognizes the different hand gestures is very impressive. As different quick time events happen, I  was surprised at how quickly each gesture was recognized and how intuitive the gameplay became.

The game doesn’t stop at the story, as there are also mini games available to play in between save points. The game is fun and although the choice between teleport movement and continuous movement is available, moving your character via hand tracking seems to be the weakest point, and is overshadowed by how awesome performing and recognizing the hand gestures are.

reviews.review_title: Played the og drakheir and I'm kinda sad
reviews.review_description:
Was playing the OG drakheir for a while and it was decent then it got updated....was alot better overall but they got rid of the hand bandages (which was kinda lame since I sort of liked them) but that's fine......I lost my progress unfortunately but got the Q3s and started playing it again and was having a blast with it then heard a newer iteration was coming and I was initially hyped but then I played it

So overall I like some of the changes like the graphics looked slightly better, added a cool little counter spell attack for when you dodge attacks....stuff like that but they got rid of the stuff already in the game like ragdoll physics which doesn't sound that bad but now instead of having a satisfying takedown after killing them now they just do super simple and janky animations like they fly up then land flat on the floor (sometimes they land on the floor in the air which looks really janky) with ragdoll physics it's always random which makes it more realistic but a simple animation is gonna get old and after playing it for a hour and a half or so it got old really quick....plus one of the things I liked to do was use the combo air spell and ragdoll them up in the air then take out the other opponents waiting for them to land
(Also the first 10 to 15 mins in the starting bit of the game with the dragon the audio was so degraded and I heard crackling sounds the whole time till I got to the first island with the story scroll, sounded like it was gonna break my speakers)

They also got rid of the main moving system I used which was the head lean, wasn't perfect and needed a little bit of work but was way better then the other two options since teleporting is lame and the other one involves way to much hand movements on top of you trying to cast spells as well (its better that you can slide now but grabbing the air to move still isnt the best)....I'm gonna play it a bit longer but I doubt I'll be able to truly like it without the movement setting and ragdoll physics not being in the game plus I feel like my favorite combination spells aren't gonna be in this version, thankfully I have the OG version so I guess I'll just settle with that unfortunately unless the newer version does get the movement settings and ragdoll physics back but I doubt it....

I know the devs probably won't see this but getting rid of the ragdoll physics was definitely not the best decision since it felt fresher then seeing the same two to three animations over and over....regular games not having ragdoll physics is pretty noticeable but in vr it is super noticeable (I doubt it was for performance or anything since the game has the same performance consistency as the OG drakheir so I'm still kinda stumped on why they removed a good feature that didn't need to be touched)

I might refund but I wanna give it a chance since I actually really loved the first one and how it felt using the magic....still kinda bummed out features in the game got removed when they were fine in the first place
(If these things come back and I like the combat later on like the OG drakheir I might change my review/rating on the game)

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate you taking the time to share such a detailed and thoughtful review. It’s clear that you’ve been with Drakheir for a while, and we’re grateful for your support from the original version through to the latest iteration.  

We understand your disappointment about certain features being changed or removed, and we hear you loud and clear, especially regarding ragdoll physics, movement options, and some of the spell mechanics. Our goal with the update was to refine and improve the experience, but we now realize that some of these changes impacted the fun and immersion in ways we didn’t intend.  

Regarding movement, we’re always looking at ways to improve control options to make the game feel intuitive while keeping spellcasting smooth. Your feedback on head-lean movement and the air-grab system is very helpful, and we’ll take it into account as we continue to refine things.  

The audio issues you mentioned, particularly in the intro sequence, are definitely concerning, and we’ll investigate them to ensure they don’t affect immersion.  

As for ragdoll physics, we genuinely appreciate your perspective on how it made combat more dynamic. While we had our reasons for adjusting how enemy deaths are handled, we’ll strongly consider bringing back ragdolls in some form to restore that sense of impact and variation.  

We hope you’ll stick around and give us the chance to improve things. We have an update coming soon, and we’d love for you to revisit the game once it’s live. If you have any additional feedback or suggestions, we’d love to hear from you directly on our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Thanks again for your support, and we truly appreciate your passion for the game.

reviews.review_description:
Playing Drakheir was a refreshing experience.
The controller free gameplay felt innovative and really immersive. I enjoyed the hand tracking mechanics, even though some gestures like the ice magic attack felt a bit awkward and made my hands uncomfortable after a while. Still the overall experience was super engaging and the freedom of movement added a unique touch. Definitely worth trying for anyone looking for something different in VR!
Every time I dodge a attack it still hits I've been on the first level for 5 hours and the shop won't open every time I move my hand it stops working for a few seconds I want it refunded

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear that you’ve had such a frustrating experience with the game. As a small team, we’ve worked extremely hard on Drakheir, and since this is our first title, we are constantly learning and improving.  

We understand how important responsive dodging and smooth gameplay are, and we’re actively working on fixes and optimizations. We actually have a major update coming out very soon that addresses several gameplay mechanics, including improvements to tracking, combat, and overall performance.  

We would truly appreciate it if you could revisit the game after the update and give it another shot. If you’re still experiencing issues, please reach out to us on Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU so we can help troubleshoot and ensure you get the best possible experience.  

Your feedback is invaluable to us, and we hope to change your mind with the improvements we’re making. Thank you for giving Drakheir a try!

reviews.review_title: The game has a huge issue of glitches.
reviews.review_description: i pre-ordered this game and waited so long for this game to release since September. I found it fun. But theres a lot of issues that the developers most take care of!! Im playing on meta quest 3 and i dont think its supposed to be glitchy as this. If the game doesnt get fixed ill just stop playing.. you rushed for this game to released but doesnt pay attention to its glitches is a bit disappointing. But i hope this game gets better performance and better quality gameplay. The hand tracking are quite impressive but sometimes they don’t really react to the movement of spells as they should. But its fine. I hope anyone will found this review helpful because i dont think im the only one who struggles with this issues.

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate you pre-ordering Drakheir and sticking with us from the start. It means a lot to us that you see the potential in the game, and we completely understand your frustration with the glitches and performance issues.

That being said, we have a major update coming very soon that directly addresses many of the concerns you've mentioned. This update will bring significant performance improvements, better hand-tracking responsiveness, and major bug fixes to ensure a smoother and more immersive experience, especially on Meta Quest 3. We've been working incredibly hard to refine movement, spellcasting accuracy, and overall stability, and we truly believe this update will bring Drakheir closer to the game we envisioned from the start.

As a small team developing our first title, we’ve been carefully listening to player feedback, and this upcoming patch is just the beginning. We're committed to continuous updates that further optimize the game, enhance combat depth, and polish every aspect of the experience.

If you have specific issues you've encountered, we’d love to hear more details so we can ensure they’re fully addressed in the update. Feel free to join our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU to share your feedback directly with us and stay up to date on patch notes.

We really hope you’ll revisit Drakheir once this update is live and see the improvements for yourself. We’re pouring everything we have into making this game the best it can be, and we’d love for you to give it another shot.

reviews.review_description: The thing that I do like about this game is the creative use of hand tracking to cast spells. Different hand signs often appear near the enemies, and it feels good to do a bunch of them fast. This system has a lot of potential imo. Everything else about the game feels generic. The game would be a lot better if the enemies were interesting. If you really like roguelikes I think you could enjoy this game just fine.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts! We’re glad to hear you’re enjoying the unique hand-tracking spellcasting system, it’s one of the features we’re most proud of, and we agree it has a lot of potential.  

We totally understand your point about the game feeling generic in other areas. We’re actively working on adding more depth to the enemies, gameplay variety, and overall experience to make the game feel more engaging. We want Drakheir to stand out not only with its hand-tracking mechanics but also through compelling combat, world-building, and interesting challenges.  

We’d love for you to check out future updates, as we’re constantly improving and expanding the game. If you have any specific suggestions on how we could make the enemies or other aspects of the game more interesting, we’d love to hear your feedback. Please feel free to join us on Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Your input is valuable as we continue to grow the game. Thank you for your support!

reviews.review_description: This is like an early access. Great potential poor execution. Refund.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate you giving Drakheir a try and understand your frustration. As a small team working on our first title, we’ve put a lot of passion into creating a unique hand-tracking RPG, but we recognize that there’s always room for improvement.  

We have a major update coming very soon that addresses many issues, including bug fixes, performance improvements, and overall polish. We’d love for you to revisit the game after the update and see how far it has come.  

If you have specific feedback on what felt unpolished or what would make the experience better for you, we’d love to hear from you on our Discord at https://discord.gg/aN5FgkPqCU Your input helps us refine the game, and we truly hope to change your mind. Thank you for trying Drakheir!

reviews.review_title: Literally Magic at Your Finger Tips
reviews.review_description:
Drakheir isn’t just a game, it’s an experience like no other. It’s the kind of game that sets a whole new standard, showing how awesome a game can be when it’s all about using just your hands. This is the type of game everyone’s gonna want to copy and be compared to.
From the start, you’re thrown into this crazy fantasy world with fairies and ogre monsters everywhere. You begin with just one magic power, but soon you unlock more, and combining them into these insane spells is where the real fun starts. You can wipe out enemies with just a few hand motions, it’s like you’re actually casting magic. Drakheir’s magic system is so unique. Each power has its own hand gesture, and once you’ve got a bunch of them, things get wild. Learning the gestures can be a little tough at first, but once you’ve got it down, you’ll feel like Gandalf the Grey. Plus, the game helps you out by showing the spells on the screen, so if you forget a gesture, you can just look down.
My first jaw dropping moment was right at the beginning when I learned how to cast this epic magical atomic bomb meteor spell. Watching it obliterate an enemy was insane and set the mood for the entire game.
Traversal is another thing that’s super cool. It feels weird at first, but once you get used to it, you’ll love it and won’t want it any other way. The story and lore are also really interesting. There are pages scattered around that give you more details about what’s going on, and I couldn’t stop hunting them down.
When I finished the main story, I immediately started another playthrough to learn all the spells I missed. I’ve never done that with any game before, but it was 100% worth it.

reviews.review_title: love the concept and how it played out!
reviews.review_description:
the concept is cool having abilities like each hand sign can be something and even opening the settings you have to a hand sign. I love the fusions in this game how you get 2 different abilities and fuse them together to get like a fire + thunder to get a beam to kill the enemies for example.
and when a boss is about to die you have to do multiple types of hand gestures like Naruto style.
although the story was a bit complicated it was still a great experience
would recommend 👍
This game was neat with the hand tracking in order to cast spells. It felt like I was in a fantasy world learning magic myself.

Free Key given to test by Impact Labs

Drakheir is, to put it quite simply a very fun game!

There is something very special about being able to put down your controllers and forget about them while in an immersive VR world where all you need are your hands!

It feels very cool and powerful during combat to blast a variety of spell attacks at enemies, from quick fire balls to longer combos and elemental spell fusions to spice things up! Then there are the finishing moves!!!

The game in its current state though is not without its issues. There is definitely room for more fine tuning to the storyline mode (Some cutscenes, voice acting and dialogue weren't 100% polished) and some increases to the difficulty in the challenge roguelike mode (It was so easy it got a bit dull after a couple of hours doing the same run!!!)

I also felt that the tutorial process could be streamlined somewhat as there are multiple places you need to go to to learn all the moves and various hand gestures fully outside of the story mode.

I will say though that the developers have worked hard to produce a very exciting set of core mechanics based around hand gestures rather than the typical 'wand waving' that many magic based games use. These hand gestures need to be learned over time for the fullest experience and that challenge combined with the variety of spells available and the different game modes will keep us VR fans returning for more of the action!

Oh and there are wack a mole and snowball fights as mini games!! Yup! Awesome!

Overall this game has earned a cool four stars from me as I would like to see a bit more refinement and polish in a few areas. However if you want an adrenaline fueled sorcery combat game then this is definitely for you ❤️

reviews.review_title: Ninja action in a fantasy setting
reviews.review_description:
It is incredibly fun to unleash powerful spells with just your hands, I especially enjoy the Challenge Mode where you stack skill over skill and just get more and more powerful over time. However this game is still in need of polishing performance wise and partially controls wise in a way that movement controls wouldn't conflict with certain skills (such as moving with sword/axe/shield and turning with fire).
That aside it is incredibly fun and great to get rid of some excess energy!

reviews.review_title: Casting spells like a mage or a ninja
reviews.review_description: I have never enjoyed a magic casting game quite like this one, where all those spells you cast as a child actaually take form in front of your eyes. Froom shooting fireballs to throwing ice shurikens or even creating as many summons as you can this game has multiple spells for you to try out. The coolest thing about this by far is that any spells you create in game you can recreate in real life since it is your hands it tracks. Though the story and game themselves aren't the most refined it's the experience of dropping meteorites on your enemies that mattered to me, and I thoroughly enjoyed learning and trying out every spell I could master.

Gotta give this gesture VR game a 5-star review! After entering the game, the setting that can release all kinds of cool skills through gestures is really amazing, as if I am a magician with magical powers, every time I wave my palm, change gestures, and the corresponding skills roar out to attack monsters, and the sense of immersion is full. The game's controls are very responsive, with no lag at all, making the release of skills smooth and accurate. And the design of the monsters is also distinctive, and the battle scenes are rich, and the more you play, the more exciting it becomes. It feels like it really takes the fun of VR interaction to the extreme, and it is highly recommended for friends who like an immersive gaming experience!
If you like Naruto you’re going love this game I’m addictive to it can’t stop playing

I find it interesting to cast spells and fight using gestures. I had a great time at the beginning. However, later on, it became a bit difficult because the motion gestures weren't very sensitive. I hope there will be improvements in this regard.

Darkheir is a short fun hand-tracking game where you cast spells with hand gestures. I played on a Quest 3 and the hand-tracking was amazing! For example, you make the finger-point gesture to summon fireballs that you can shoot at the enemy with a flick of the wrist. Then, when the enemy is weak, you cast an ultimate fireball by making ninja like gestures. Overall, the hand-tracking content was amazing! But, that's not all, you can buy new spells like ice and wind to do even more magic!

The 3D models reminded me of older nintendo games; however, the particle effects for all of the spells were amazing and detailed! And, best of all, it was optimized and did not lag my Quest 3 at all! The story was decent and had subtitles and some voice lines. The mini-games, like whack-a-mole and snowball fight were a bit underwhelming; however, the main campaign was very fun.

Overall, I'd say it has a lot of potential and if you want a fun game to try out the improved Quest 3 hand-tracking, you should definately pick this game up.
It's a fun game but the way you move I can't move where I want to I always go flying around I've tried all the ways to move I can think of just didn't work for me good game other wise

I highly recommend you to play this game. I came back to write reviews after defeating the high Priest and the fairy princess, the game is really fun, gesture magic can be constantly learned to upgrade, the battle is very refreshing.
Another point, if you carefully read the game story and some diaries, will you find that the protagonist is really righteous?

developer_response.body: Hey there! We’re really glad to see you exploring the lore. Our story's main message is about being “anti-war,” and we’d love to hear your thoughts on it! Feel free to join our friendly Discord community—we can’t wait to chat and share ideas with you! https://discord.com/invite/SmEQk93M5E

reviews.review_description: A great game, very fun, with which it would be easy to pass the time. But which with a few small improvements and updates would be really perfect

reviews.review_title: What I wanted for a long time
reviews.review_description:
I wished for a long time that we had a game for vr like this, in which you can cast spells by some kind of movement not just pressing some buttons. And finally we have that and it's even better because it's done through hand gestures. I had so much fun. There are different spells and gestures you need to learn, and it makes you feel like you're some kind of wizard or witch. 
Although hands gestures work great most of the times, if devs improve it a bit, it would be flawless. 
I'm looking forward to new updates and improvements.
